skill.name: database
skill.description: A database workflow guide for schema design, migrations, query optimization, data pipelines, data quality, and operations.
skill.summary: Coordinate database design, implementation, optimization, migration, pipeline, quality, and operations work.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and prose text as weak cryptography. Manual review found documentation-only skill references, copy-paste prompts, and checklist text in SKILL.md, with no executable scripts, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence. The static findings are false positives and the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis classified Markdown inline code and fenced prompt examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations contain documentation, skill names, and copy-paste prompts, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown bullets or fenced text blocks in SKILL.md. No script file, command runner, shell syntax, or execution mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detector Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in descriptive database workflow prose. The relevant lines discuss database design, schemas, data pipelines, and checklists, not cryptographic functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspected lines contain ordinary words such as design and database workflow text. There is no cryptographic API usage, algorithm implementation, key handling, or hashing code.

content.value_statement: Database projects often fail when design, migrations, performance, and operations are handled separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for planning and coordinating database work.
actual_capabilities: Organizes database work into design, implementation, optimization, migration, pipeline, quality, and operations phases.
Lists related specialist skills for PostgreSQL, NoSQL, Prisma, migrations, data engineering, and administration.
Provides copy-paste prompts for invoking database architecture, ORM, migration, optimization, and operations support.
Defines action checklists for schema planning, migrations, performance tuning, data quality, and backup readiness.
Maps common technologies such as PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and data warehouses to relevant workflow skills.
limitations: It is a coordination guide and does not execute database commands directly.
It depends on referenced specialist skills being available in the user's environment.
It does not validate schemas, migrations, or queries by itself.
It does not include vendor-specific setup steps for every database platform.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Application Database
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to gather requirements, design schema structure, choose indexes, and prepare implementation prompts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate a Production Migration
use_cases.description: Use the migration phase to plan scripts, tests, execution steps, and data integrity checks before release.

use_cases.title: Improve Data Platform Reliability
use_cases.description: Use the pipeline, quality, and operations phases to add monitoring, validation, backups, and documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Start Database Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new schema plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database workflow to plan a schema for my application. Ask for missing requirements before recommending tables, relationships, and indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Migration Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for controlled database changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the database migration phase to create a migration plan. Include preparation, test data, rollback checks, execution steps, and verification.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Slow Queries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the query optimization phase to review these slow queries. Identify likely causes, index changes, query rewrites, and measurement steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design End-to-End Data Operations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request across pipelines, quality, and operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full database workflow to design a production data operations plan. Cover pipelines, quality gates, monitoring, backups, ownership, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Plan a database for a small marketplace app.
output: A requirements checklist for products, users, orders, payments, and audit events.
A schema planning outline with relationships, indexes, migration order, and seed data needs.
Suggested specialist prompts for architecture, PostgreSQL design, and ORM setup.

output_examples.input: Prepare a migration from a legacy customer table.
output: A migration strategy with staging, validation, rollback, and data integrity checks.
A testing checklist for row counts, constraints, application compatibility, and monitoring.
Recommended follow-up prompts for migration planning and database administration.

output_examples.input: Improve reliability for daily analytics jobs.
output: A data pipeline plan covering ingestion, transformations, scheduling, and monitoring.
A data quality checklist for validation rules, alerts, documentation, and ownership.
Suggested prompts for data engineering, Airflow workflows, and dbt transformation patterns.

best_practices: Start with requirements and data ownership before choosing tables, indexes, or tools.
Test migrations and rollback plans against realistic data before production changes.
Pair performance work with measurements, execution plans, and clear success thresholds.
anti_patterns: Do not use the workflow as a replacement for database backups or access controls.
Do not run generated migration plans in production without review and testing.
Do not assume every referenced specialist skill is installed or available.

faq.question: Does this skill run SQL commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide that helps plan and coordinate database work.

faq.question: Can it design both SQL and NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes phases and specialist prompts for SQL, PostgreSQL, and NoSQL design.

faq.question: Does it create migration scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning prompts and checklists for migration work.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it useful for data engineering?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes phases for data pipelines, transformations, quality checks, and monitoring.

faq.question: What should I verify before production use?
faq.answer: Verify backups, migration tests, rollback plans, performance benchmarks, monitoring, and documentation.
